info.title: WIP Def-Store Service
info.description:
## World In a Pie - Terminology & Ontology Management

The Def-Store service manages controlled vocabularies (terminologies) for the WIP ecosystem.

### Key Features

- **Terminology Management**: Create and manage controlled vocabularies
- **Term Management**: Add, update, deprecate terms within terminologies
- **Validation API**: Validate values against terminologies
- **Import/Export**: JSON and CSV support for bulk operations
- **Multi-language**: Translation support for internationalization
- **Hierarchical Terms**: Support for parent-child relationships
- **Namespace Isolation**: Multi-tenant data isolation

### Authentication

All endpoints require API key authentication via the `X-API-Key` header.

### Integration with Registry

Terminologies and terms are registered with the WIP Registry service to get
unique, system-wide identifiers. IDs are configurable per namespace (default: UUID7).

post.description:
Create one or more terminologies (controlled vocabularies).

Each terminology will be registered with the Registry service to get
a unique ID. Namespace is specified per item (default: "wip").

get.description: List all terminologies with pagination and optional filters.

put.description:
Update one or more terminologies.

If a value changes, a synonym will be added in the Registry to allow
lookups by both old and new values.

delete.description:
Soft-delete one or more terminologies (set status to inactive).

All terms in each terminology will also be deactivated.
Set force=true per item to delete even if templates reference it.

get.description: Get a terminology by its value (e.g., DOC_STATUS).

parameters.name: namespace

schema.description: Namespace to search in (omit for all)
schema.title: Namespace

parameters.description: Namespace to search in (omit for all)

get.description:
Get a terminology by ID.

The ID can be either the Registry ID or the value (DOC_STATUS).
When falling back to value lookup, namespace is used for scoping.

Get dependencies of a terminology.

Returns information about what depends on this terminology:
- Templates that reference it via terminology_ref fields

Use this to check before deactivating a terminology.

post.description:
Restore a soft-deleted (inactive) terminology back to active status.

By default also reactivates all terms that were deactivated with it.
Set restore_terms=false to restore only the terminology itself.

Create one or more terms in a terminology.

Namespace is inherited from the parent terminology.
For single items, uses direct creation. For multiple items, uses
optimized batch operations.

For very large imports (100k+ terms), you may need to tune the batch sizes:
- `batch_size`: Controls MongoDB batch size (default 1000)
- `registry_batch_size`: Controls registry HTTP call batch size (default 100)

parameters.name: batch_size

schema.description: Number of terms per MongoDB batch (default 1000)
schema.title: Batch Size

parameters.description: Number of terms per MongoDB batch (default 1000)

parameters.name: registry_batch_size

schema.description: Number of terms per registry HTTP call (default 100). Reduce if experiencing timeouts on large imports.
schema.title: Registry Batch Size

parameters.description: Number of terms per registry HTTP call (default 100). Reduce if experiencing timeouts on large imports.

get.description: List terms in a terminology with pagination.
Get a term by its ID.

delete.description: Soft-delete one or more terms (set status to inactive).

post.description:
Deprecate one or more terms.

Deprecated terms are kept for historical data but marked as deprecated.
Optionally specify a replacement term per item.

Validate a value against a terminology.

Returns whether the value is valid and the matching term if found.
Also provides suggestions for close matches.

get.description:
Export a terminology with all its terms.

Supports JSON and CSV formats. Use include_relationships=true to include
ontology relationships (is_a, part_of, etc.) in JSON exports.
Export all terminologies with their terms.

post.description:
Import a terminology with terms.

JSON format expected:
```json
{
  "terminology": {
    "value": "DOC_STATUS",
    "label": "Document Status",
    "description": "...",
    "case_sensitive": false
  },
  "terms": [
    {"value": "draft", "label": "Draft"},
    {"value": "approved", "label": "Approved"}
  ]
}
```

CSV format requires terminology_value and terminology_label in the data,
plus csv_content with columns: value, label, description, sort_order

For very large imports (100k+ terms), you may need to tune the batch sizes:
- `batch_size`: Controls MongoDB batch size (default 1000)
- `registry_batch_size`: Controls registry HTTP call batch size (default 100)

If you experience timeouts, try reducing `registry_batch_size` to 50 or lower.

parameters.name: batch_size

schema.description: Number of terms per MongoDB batch (default 1000)
schema.title: Batch Size

parameters.description: Number of terms per MongoDB batch (default 1000)

parameters.name: registry_batch_size

schema.description: Number of terms per registry HTTP call (default 100). Reduce if experiencing timeouts on large imports.
schema.title: Registry Batch Size

parameters.description: Number of terms per registry HTTP call (default 100). Reduce if experiencing timeouts on large imports.

post.description:
Import an OBO Graph JSON ontology (HP, GO, CHEBI, etc.).

Accepts standard OBO Graph JSON format with `graphs[0].nodes[]` and
`graphs[0].edges[]`. Parses nodes into terms and edges into relationships.

Auto-detects the ontology prefix and metadata from the graph structure.
For large ontologies, use the CLI script `scripts/import_obo_graph.py` instead.

parameters.name: terminology_value

schema.description: WIP terminology value (e.g., HPO, GO). Auto-detected if not set.
schema.title: Terminology Value

parameters.description: WIP terminology value (e.g., HPO, GO). Auto-detected if not set.

parameters.name: terminology_label

schema.description: Display label. Auto-detected if not set.
schema.title: Terminology Label

parameters.description: Display label. Auto-detected if not set.

parameters.name: prefix_filter

schema.description: Only import nodes with this OBO prefix
schema.title: Prefix Filter

parameters.description: Only import nodes with this OBO prefix

post.description:
Import a terminology from a URL.

Fetches the data from the URL and imports it.

For very large imports (100k+ terms), you may need to tune the batch sizes:
- `batch_size`: Controls MongoDB batch size (default 1000)
- `registry_batch_size`: Controls registry HTTP call batch size (default 100)

parameters.name: batch_size

schema.description: Number of terms per MongoDB batch (default 1000)
schema.title: Batch Size

parameters.description: Number of terms per MongoDB batch (default 1000)

parameters.name: registry_batch_size

schema.description: Number of terms per registry HTTP call (default 100). Reduce if experiencing timeouts on large imports.
schema.title: Registry Batch Size

parameters.description: Number of terms per registry HTTP call (default 100). Reduce if experiencing timeouts on large imports.

post.description:
Validate a single value against a terminology.

Provide either terminology_id or terminology_value (not both).

get.summary: Get audit log for a term
get.description: Retrieve the complete change history for a specific term.

get.summary: Get audit log for a terminology
get.description: Retrieve the change history for all terms in a terminology.

post.description:
Create one or more typed relationships between terms.

Relationship types include: is_a, part_of, has_part, maps_to, related_to,
finding_site, causative_agent, or any custom type.

get.description: List relationships for a term, with optional direction and type filtering.

parameters.name: term_id

schema.description: Term ID to query relationships for
schema.title: Term Id

parameters.description: Term ID to query relationships for

delete.description: Soft-delete one or more relationships (set status to inactive).

get.description:
List all relationships in a namespace (paginated).

Unlike the per-term list endpoint, this returns ALL relationships,
useful for batch sync and export operations.

Traverse upward from a term, following outgoing relationships of the given type.

For is_a relationships, also follows parent_term_id links for backward
compatibility with simple hierarchical terminologies.

Traverse downward from a term, following incoming relationships of the given type.

For is_a relationships, also includes children via parent_term_id.

get.summary: Get direct parents of a term
get.description:
Get immediate parents of a term (non-transitive).

Combines is_a relationships and parent_term_id.

schema.title: Response Get Parents Api Def Store Ontology Terms  Term Id  Parents Get

get.summary: Get direct children of a term
get.description:
Get immediate children of a term (non-transitive).

Combines incoming is_a relationships and children via parent_term_id.

schema.title: Response Get Children Api Def Store Ontology Terms  Term Id  Children Get

get.description: Root endpoint with service information.

Health check endpoint.

Verifies MongoDB and Registry connectivity.

Readiness check endpoint.

Returns 200 when the service is ready to accept traffic.
